front: What is the definition of the order relation ≤ between two cardinal numbers?
back: For two sets A and B, we say that the cardinal number |A| is less than or equal to the cardinal number |B|, written |A| ≤ |B|, if there exists an injective function from A into B.

front: What does the Cantor-Schröder-Bernstein Theorem state regarding the order relation of cardinal numbers?
back: If |A| ≤ |B| and |B| ≤ |A|, then |A| = |B|. This means that if there is an injection from A to B and an injection from B to A, then there is a bijection between A and B, and the sets have the same cardinality.

front: What is the strict order relation < between two cardinal numbers?
back: We say |A| < |B| if |A| ≤ |B| but it is not the case that |B| ≤ |A|. In other words, there is an injection from A to B, but no injection (and hence no bijection) from B to A.

front: State the Law of Trichotomy (or Well-Ordering Principle) for cardinal numbers.
back: For any two cardinal numbers 𝔞 and 𝔟, exactly one of the following holds: 𝔞 < 𝔟, 𝔞 = 𝔟, or 𝔟 < 𝔞. This is equivalent to the Axiom of Choice.

front: How is the order relation for finite cardinals related to the order relation for natural numbers?
back: For finite sets, the order relation on cardinal numbers coincides with the usual order relation on the natural numbers. If A has n elements and B has m elements, then |A| ≤ |B| if and only if n ≤ m as natural numbers.
